title: A metropolis designing its way out of the heat
dek: Abidjan is mapping shade, wind and tree cover as essential urban services.
author: Aya Konan
body: On Abidjan’s hottest streets, the difference between one block and the next can be several degrees. A new city map makes those invisible inequalities visible.
Planners are using the data to prioritise trees, market canopies and cooler walking routes near schools and transit stops.
Heat adaptation is becoming part of daily city management—a public health intervention measured in shade, distance and time.
